subject: Remove uses of deprecated library JMockit in test classes (catalog-be)
commit_message:
Remove uses of deprecated library JMockit in test classes (catalog-be)

Issue-ID: SDC-3379

Change-Id: If5c6b55d24bdd9a8298089407ab97405f0ca439e
Signed-off-by: Bastien Müllers <bastien.mullers@intl.att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/sdc-master-verify-java/8849/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/sdc-master-verify-java/8849

Patch Set 1:

I have removed all remaining uses of the deprecated Mockit.Deencapsulation library.
Unfortunately this has resulted in insufficient code coverage ( < 55% on sonarqube).

I don't know the project very well and am currently struggling with adding more unit tests to counterbalance the decreased coverage.
If anyone knows a good place for adding new unit tests I'll be glad to look into it.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I have removed all remaining uses of the deprecated Mockit.Deencapsulation library.
> Unfortunately this has resulted in insufficient code coverage ( < 55% on sonarqube).
> 
> I don't know the project very well and am currently struggling with adding more unit tests to counterbalance the decreased coverage.
> If anyone knows a good place for adding new unit tests I'll be glad to look into it.

In this case would be good to rewrite the tests that are using Mockit.Deencapsulation before delete it.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > I have removed all remaining uses of the deprecated Mockit.Deencapsulation library.
> > Unfortunately this has resulted in insufficient code coverage ( < 55% on sonarqube).
> > 
> > I don't know the project very well and am currently struggling with adding more unit tests to counterbalance the decreased coverage.
> > If anyone knows a good place for adding new unit tests I'll be glad to look into it.
> 
> In this case would be good to rewrite the tests that are using Mockit.Deencapsulation before delete it.

Agree, and this should be done from case to case, I mean, from test class to test class in separate commits. Removing so many test from many different classes at once, without a proper replacement, is not recommended.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Totally agree with Andre and Andreson.

For instance:
Some of the removed tests (private methods) probably covered by public methods, in this case it's OK to remove, 
else, you should create tests covering public methods and mock conditions to call private one.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Totally agree with Andre and Andreson.
> 
> For instance:
> Some of the removed tests (private methods) probably covered by public methods, in this case it's OK to remove, 
> else, you should create tests covering public methods and mock conditions to call private one.

Thanks for the feedback.
I will discard these changes and start over in a new commit, following your remarks.
